revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:風土記の丘]]
[[File:Oni-no-iwaya kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Saitobaru Fudoki Hill in Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture, developed as the first Fudoki Hill project. The photo shows Kofun No. 206 (Oni no Iwaya Kofun).]] [[File:20090421下侍塚古墳.jpg|thumb|210px|Nasu Fudoki Hill, including {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousamurai Kofun|qq=}} on the terrace of the Naka River.]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Tumulus In Spring 1.jpg|thumb|209x209px|Sakitama Fudoki Hill, featuring {{ill|Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun|en|Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun|qid=Q11371449|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|1=Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun}}.]] [[File:Iwaya-kofun-zenkei.jpg|thumb|210px|Bōsō Fudoki Hill, home to {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture.]] [[File:Azuchi.jpg|thumb|210px|Ōmi Fudoki Hill, including {{ill|Azuchi Castle|en|Azuchi Castle|qid=Q786888|lt=Azuchi Castle ruins|1=Azuchi Castle}}.]] [[File:赤塚古墳01.jpg|thumb|210px|Usa Fudoki Hill, featuring {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|lt=Aka-tsuka Kofun|ja|赤塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aka-tsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川部・高森古墳群}}.]] '''Fudoki Hill''' (風土記の丘) is an outdoor museum and historical park established around historical sites, including {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}, based on the Fudoki Hill installation concept by the Agency for Cultural Affairs. Its purpose is to preserve and utilize archaeological sites and historical materials.
== Overview == According to the "Fudoki Hill Installation Guidelines" (see [[#External links|External links]]), Fudoki Hill aims to preserve archaeological sites, such as kofun (ancient tumuli) and castle ruins, while integrating their preservation with that of historical materials stored and displayed in dedicated museums.
The Agency for Cultural Affairs announced the Fudoki Hill installation concept in 1966,<ref>[https://www.mext.go.jp/b_menu/hakusho/html/others/detail/1318560.htm Development and Utilization of Historic Sites]</ref> and the first Fudoki Hill, "Saitobaru Fudoki Hill," opened the same year.<ref>{{Cite web|author=General Incorporated Foundation Miyazaki Park Association|url=https://mppf.or.jp/saito/about/|title=About the Park|work=Saitobaru Kofun Group Special Historic Site Park|accessdate=2024-03-17}}</ref> Subsequent developments included "Sakitama Fudoki Hill" in 1967, "Ki no Kuni Fudoki Hill" in 1968, "Tateyama Fudoki Hill" in 1969, and "Azuchi Fudoki Hill" the same year. As of 2007, 16 Fudoki Hills have been established across Japan.
== List of Fudoki Hills ==
* {{ill|Ukitamu Fudoki Hill|ja|山形県立うきたむ風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukitamu Fudoki Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takahata Town, Higashiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture) ** Museum: Yamagata Prefectural Ukitamu Fudoki Hill Archaeological Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Hinata Caves|en|Hinata Caves|qid=Q24864065|lt=Hinata Caves|1=Hinata Caves}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City, Tochigi Prefecture) ** Museum: Tochigi Prefectural Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins|ja|下野国分寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunniji ruins|ja|下野国分尼寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kokubunniji ruins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野国分寺跡|1=Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361382}} * Nasu Fudoki Hill (Nakagawa Town and Ōtawara City, Nasu District, Tochigi Prefecture) ** Museum: Tochigi Prefectural Nasu Fudoki Hill Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousamurai Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakitama Fudoki Hill|ja|さきたま古墳公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Gyōda City, Saitama Prefecture) ** Museum: Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Historic Site Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|lt=Sakitama Kofun Group|ja|さきたま古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉古墳群}} * {{ill|Bōsō Fudoki Hill|ja|千葉県立房総のむら|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Sakae Town, Inba District, and Narita City, Chiba Prefecture) ** Museum: Chiba Prefectural Bōsō Fudoki Hill Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, Former Gakushūin Elementary School Hall, Former Mikogami Family Residence * Tateyama Fudoki Hill (Tateyama Town, Nakaniikawa District, Toyama Prefecture) ** Museum: Toyama Prefectural Tateyama Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|en|Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|qid=Q11659204|lt=Oyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)}}, Former Shima Family Residence, Enma Hall, Former Arima Family Residence with Taya Gate, Kyōsanbō and Zendōbō ({{ill|Shukubo|en|Shukubo|qid=Q11455614|lt=Shukubō|1=Shukubo}}) * {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|ja|曽根丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Kōfu City, Yamanashi Prefecture) ** Museum: Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qid=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} * Ōmi Fudoki Hill (Azuchi Town, Ōmihachiman City, Shiga Prefecture) ** Museum: Shiga Prefectural Azuchi Castle Archaeological Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Azuchi Castle|en|Azuchi Castle|qid=Q786888|lt=Azuchi Castle ruins|1=Azuchi Castle}}, {{ill|Kannonji Castle|en|Kannonji Castle|qid=Q2969462|lt=Kannonji Castle ruins|1=Kannonji Castle}}, {{ill|Azuchi-Hyōtanyama Kofun|en|Azuchi-Hyōtanyama Kofun|qid=Q11450459|lt=Hyōtan Mountain Kofun|1=Azuchi-Hyōtanyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Kanan Town, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture) ** Museum: Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|en|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11353283|lt=Ichisuka Kofun Group|1=Ichisuka Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|en|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|qid=Q11417470|lt=Wakayama Prefectural Ki Fudoki Hill|1=Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore}} (Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture) ** Museum: Ki Fudoki Hill Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11475228|lt=Iwashimizuka Kofun Group|1=Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill|ja|島根県立八雲立つ風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Matsue City, Shimane Prefecture) ** Museum: Yakumotatsu Fudoki Hill Exhibition Learning Center, Guidance Yamashiro Village ** Sites: {{ill|Izumo Kokufu ruins|ja|Izumo Province 府跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Kokufu ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okadayama Kofun|lt=Okadayama Kofun|lt=Okadayama Kofun Group|ja|岡田山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okadayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡田山古墳}} * Kibi Road Fudoki Hill (Sōja City, Okayama Prefecture) ** Museum: Okayama Prefectural Kibi Road Folk Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Bitchū Kokubun-ji|en|Bitchū Kokubun-ji|qid=Q10889244|lt=Bitchū Kokubunji|1=Bitchū Kokubun-ji}} * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Miyoshi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) ** Museum: Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore ** Sites: Jōrakuji and Nanatsuzuka Kofun Group, Former Shinno Residence * {{ill|Higo Kodai no Mori|ja|肥後古代の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Kodai no Mori|qq=}} (Yamaga City and Nankan Town, Tamana District, Kumamoto Prefecture) ** Museum: Kumamoto Prefectural Museum of Decorative Kofuns ** Sites: Iwahara Kofun Group * {{ill|Usa Fudoki Hill|ja|宇佐風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Usa City, Ōita Prefecture) ** Museum: Ōita Prefectural History Museum ** Sites: Kawabe and Takamori Kofun Groups * Saitobaru Fudoki Hill (Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture) ** Museum: Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum ** Sites: {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}
Additionally, there are outdoor museums and parks named Fudoki Hill, such as Hitachi Fudoki Hill (Ishioka City, Ibaraki Prefecture) and Iki Fudoki Hill (Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture), but it is unclear if these are officially part of the Agency for Cultural Affairs’ Fudoki Hill project.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|qid=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}
== External links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20100331055852/http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/top-page/hudoki.html Fudoki Hill Installation Guidelines]
